binaries.title: hvsocket.sys - Velocity feature gate removed: guest-reachable listener accept-state validation (and loopback lifetime handling) promoted from a staged feature to unconditional

changed_functions.name: VmbusTlProviderListen
key_changes: Largest structural diff, but it is the same Velocity feature-gate fold plus churn: two EvaluateCurrentState() gates removed, keeping the feature-enabled listen path.
Feature-enabled path enforces the base-endpoint requirement early (if (v5 >= 0) -> 0xC0000010 'Required base endpoint not present', trace 191) and uses the 3-way refcount-release cleanup.
Incidental churn: VmbusTlCreateListenEndpoint inlined to VmbusTlCreateEndpoint(...,3,...); ExchangeAdd(-1)->Decrement64 codegen; trace-ordinal renumbering; Hex-Rays arg-count artifact.
changed_functions.security_notes: Host-side listen setup path. Folds to the feature-enabled branch (safer refcount/cleanup handling). No independent new bounds/validation/authorization check beyond the feature-gate fold.

changed_functions.name: VmbusTlLoopbackAcceptConnection
key_changes: Velocity feature-gate fold only. The 'reference the delivery queue first and bail 0xC000020D if NULL before VmbusTlCreateObject' behavior was ALREADY the feature-enabled path in the unpatched build (unpatched ~lines 13591-13605); it is NOT a new check.
The fold drops the feature-disabled path that read the delivery-queue pointer raw (*((_QWORD*)P + 87)) without a reference.
CORRECTION: the original report's claim that this added a new delivery-queue precondition is refuted.
changed_functions.security_notes: In-host (host-local) AF_HYPERV loopback accept, not guest-reachable. Folds to the pre-existing feature-enabled refcounted path. No independent new check introduced by the patch.

changed_functions.name: VmbusTlNotifyListenerAccept
key_changes: PRIMARY FIX. Unpatched splits on opaque predicate `EvaluateCurrentState()` (asm: `call 0x1c0001d78; test eax,eax; je 0x1c00060a0`) into two accept paths.

The `je 0x1c00060a0` branch (predicate==0) increments the inbound-connection refcount [rbx+0x130] and jumps straight to the accept-dispatch invocation WITHOUT the `cmp byte [rbx+0x128],0` listener-accept-guard check.
Only the other branch performs `cmp byte [rbx+0x128],0 / jne <error 0xC0000236>` before accepting.

Patched linearizes to a single loop that ALWAYS executes `cmp byte [rbx+0x128], 0x0 / jne 0x1c0005d18` (return 0xC0000236) before invoking the accept-dispatch callback `call qword [rel 0x1c00152b0]` = `(*(arg1[0x2a]+8))(arg1[0x2c], &var_a8)`.
changed_functions.security_notes: Velocity feature-gate fold. EvaluateCurrentState() (sub_1c0001d78) is feature 1542768955_57582472, not a race predicate. Pre-patch feature-disabled branch reaches the accept-dispatch callback without the byte[endpoint+0x128] accept-guard check; patched keeps only the checked (feature-enabled) path, returning 0xC0000236 when the guard is set. Guest-reachable (VmbusTlInboundConnectComplete). This is a state-validation hardening; the endpoint is reference-held across the callback, so no use-after-free/controlled-indirect-call is evidenced by the diff. Severity Low.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; UNPATCHED VmbusTlNotifyListenerAccept @0x1c0005f18 (key blocks; '-' lines are unpatched-only)
mov     qword [rsp+0x18], rbx
mov     qword [rsp+0x20], rsi
push    rbp
push    rdi
push    r12
push    r14
push    r15
mov     rax, qword [rel 0x1c0013090]
xor     rax, rsp
mov     qword [rbp+0x2f], rax
mov     r15, rdx
mov     rbx, rcx
call    0x1c0001d78            ; EvaluateCurrentState() opaque predicate
lea     r12, [rel 0x1c000f430]
mov     esi, 0xc0000236
lea     r14, [rel 0x1c000f468]
lea     rdi, [rbx+0x130]        ; rdi = &inbound-connection refcount (arg1[0x26])
test    eax, eax
je      0x1c00060a0            ; predicate==0 -> BYPASS branch (no [rbx+0x128] guard)
cmp     dword [rel 0x1c0013058], 0x4
jbe     0x1c00060c1
cmp     dword [rel 0x1c0013058], 0x4
jbe     0x1c0005f92
prefetchw zmmword [rdi]
mov     rax, qword [rdi]
lea     rcx, [rax+0x1]
jmp     0x1c00060de
mov     r9, qword [rdi]
mov     r8, rbx
mov     edx, 0xd3
mov     qword [rsp+0x20], r12
mov     rcx, r14
call    0x1c000bd34
prefetchw zmmword [rdi]
mov     rax, qword [rdi]
lea     rcx, [rax+0x1]
jmp     0x1c0005fab
mov     r9, qword [rdi]
mov     r8, rbx
mov     edx, 0xca
mov     qword [rsp+0x20], r12
mov     rcx, r14
call    0x1c000bd34
cmp     rcx, 0x1
ja      0x1c00060cd
cmp     rcx, 0x1
ja      0x1c0005f9e
lock cmpxchg qword [rdi], rcx  ; CAS increment refcount
mov     rcx, rax
je      0x1c0005ffa
cmp     rcx, 0x1
je      0x1c00060f1
lock cmpxchg qword [rdi], rcx
mov     rcx, rax
je      0x1c0005ff1
cmp     rcx, 0x1
je      0x1c0005fbe
; ---- accept dispatch (label 0x1c000600b) reached WITHOUT byte[rbx+0x128] check on the je-branch ----
movups  xmm0, xmmword [r15+0x88]
mov     eax, dword [r15+0xa8]
xor     edx, edx
movups  xmm1, xmmword [r15+0x98]
lea     rcx, [rbp-0x49]
mov     dword [rbp+0x27], eax
movups  xmmword [rbp+0x7], xmm0
lea     r8d, [rdx+0x50]
movups  xmmword [rbp+0x17], xmm1
call    0x1c000d740
mov     rcx, qword [rbx+0x160]
lea     rax, [rbp+0x7]
mov     qword [rbp-0x39], rax
lea     rdx, [rbp-0x49]
lea     rax, [r15+0x1e0]
mov     qword [rbp-0x29], r15
mov     qword [rbp-0x31], rax
lea     rax, [rel 0x1c000e000]  ; &VmbusTlProviderConnectDispatch
mov     qword [rbp-0x21], rax
call    qword [rel 0x1c00152b0] ; (*(arg1[0x2a]+8))(arg1[0x2c], &var_a8)  ACCEPT CALLBACK
mov     esi, eax
test    eax, eax
js      0x1c0006169
; ---- the guard check exists only on the OTHER branch: ----
cmp     byte [rbx+0x128], 0x0   ; listener accept-guard flag (arg1[0x25])
jne     0x1c0005fda            ; if set -> error 0xC0000236 (only enforced on non-bypass branch)
inc     rcx
cmp     dword [rel 0x1c0013058], 0x0
jbe     0x1c0006127
mov     ecx, 0xe
int     0x29
; PATCHED: single path always does `cmp byte [rbx+0x128],0 / jne 0x1c0005d18` before the accept callback

changed_functions.name: VmbusTlLoopbackNotifyReceiveConsumed
key_changes: Loopback receive-consumed notification restructured consistently with the loopback endpoint refcount/delivery-queue changes.
Same opaque-predicate simplification pattern; no independent new bounds/authorization check identified.

changed_functions.name: VmbusTlLoopbackSend
key_changes: Loopback send path adjusted alongside the loopback endpoint lifecycle refactor.
Refcount/trace-index adjustments; no distinct memory-safety delta beyond the shared lifecycle hardening.

changed_functions.name: VmbusTlLoopbackDisconnect
key_changes: Disconnect path updated to match new endpoint delivery-queue reference ownership.
Ensures the delivery-queue reference acquired in the patched accept is correctly released on disconnect.

changed_functions.name: VmbusTlLoopbackSetupConnection
key_changes: Loopback connection setup adjusted to initialize the endpoint delivery-queue / accept-guard fields consistently with the accept-path changes.
Minor control-flow reorganization; supports the primary fix rather than introducing an independent bug.

changed_functions.name: VmbusTlCommonConnectCompletion
key_changes: Velocity feature-gate fold: the `EvaluateCurrentState() &&` conjunct is dropped from `if (EvaluateCurrentState() && (*(_DWORD*)(a1+528) & 0x80) != 0)`.
Effect: the connection-reference release + VmbusTlQueueEndpointAction(...,9) now runs whenever the 0x80 flag is set (previously only when the feature was enabled).
Only other diff is trace ordinal 582->580.
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-gate fold, host-local completion path. Folds to the feature-enabled behavior (always run the ref-release/queue-action when the 0x80 flag is set). No independent new check.

changed_functions.name: VmbusTlProcessNewConnectionForListener
key_changes: Under the mutex, unpatched reads `if (EvaluateCurrentState()) r12 = *(endpoint+0x1a9)` then gates connection creation with `if (!EvaluateCurrentState() || r12)`.
Patched reads `rdi = *(endpoint+0x1a9)` unconditionally and gates with a hard `if (rdi)` before `VmbusTlCreateConnection`, otherwise returns error 0xC0000236 (trace ordinals 0x23c/0x243).

Adds explicit endpoint-accepting-state requirement before associating the new inbound connection to the partition (VmbusTlAssociateConnectionToPartition / VmbusTlSetEndpointId / VmbusTlSetCancellable).
changed_functions.security_notes: Velocity feature-gate fold (same feature 1542768955_57582472). Pre-patch the endpoint accepting-state byte at endpoint+0x1a9 is read and enforced only when the feature is enabled; patched reads it unconditionally under the fast mutex and hard-requires it before VmbusTlCreateConnection (0xC0000236 otherwise). Guest-reachable state-validation hardening. No memory-corruption primitive is evidenced by the diff. Severity Low.

security_findings.entry_point: Guest→host inbound AF_HYPERV/VMBus connect completion (hvsocket). Reached host-side via VmbusTlInboundConnectComplete (sub_1C00027E8) on a guest-initiated inbound connection.
call_chain: Guest issues connect() on an AF_HYPERV socket to a host-registered service GUID / listener
guest-driven inbound VMBus processing -> VmbusTlProcessNewConnection / VmbusTlProcessNewConnectionForListener
accept-dispatch callback: (*(*(endpoint+0x150)+8))(*(endpoint+0x160), &localbuf) on a reference-held endpoint
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Missing state validation on a guest-reachable accept path, gated behind a removed Velocity feature (defense-in-depth hardening)
security_findings.description: The change is the removal of a Windows Feature-Management (Velocity) staging gate, not a race fix. EvaluateCurrentState() (sub_1c0001d78) reads Velocity feature 1542768955_57582472 (EvaluateFeature -> EvaluateCurrentStateFromRegistry queries ...\Microsoft\FeatureManagement\Overrides; returns g_Feature_1542768955_57582472_cachedstate != 1). In the unpatched build VmbusTlNotifyListenerAccept has two twin paths selected by `if (EvaluateCurrentState())`: the feature-ENABLED branch checks the listener accept-guard byte at endpoint+0x128 before the accept-dispatch callback and returns 0xC0000236 (STATUS_CONNECTION_REFUSED) when set; the feature-DISABLED branch reaches the accept-dispatch callback without that check. The patched build deletes the gate and keeps only the checked (feature-enabled) path, so the guard is evaluated unconditionally. The endpoint object is reference-held across the callback (the CAS on endpoint+0x130 is a try-reference that only proceeds when the refcount is already >= 1), so this closes a state-validation gap; the diff does NOT demonstrate a use-after-free, a controlled indirect call, or any memory-corruption primitive. endpoint+0x128 is a logical accept-not-allowed/closing flag, not a liveness/free marker.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: No attacker timing trigger; the patch changes a default behavior via feature-gate removal. Behavioral delta: with the feature disabled (pre-patch), an accept-dispatch callback can fire on a listener whose accept-guard byte[endpoint+0x128] is set (not-accepting/closing state); with the feature enabled (pre-patch) and unconditionally (post-patch), the driver returns 0xC0000236 and the callback does not fire. The default enablement state of feature 1542768955_57582472 in the unpatched build is not resolvable from the decompiled code.
security_findings.primitive: Guest-reachable state-validation gap: an accept callback can be dispatched on a listener/endpoint in a non-accepting/closing state on the pre-patch feature-disabled path; now refused. No use-after-free, controlled indirect call, or memory corruption is evidenced by the diff (the endpoint is reference-held across the callback). Impact is a robustness/lifecycle-consistency issue on guest input; any higher impact is unproven and would require live analysis of the listener teardown path.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED VmbusTlNotifyListenerAccept @0x1c0005f18
call    0x1c0001d78            ; EvaluateCurrentState() opaque predicate -> eax
lea     r12, [rel 0x1c000f430]
mov     esi, 0xc0000236
lea     r14, [rel 0x1c000f468]
lea     rdi, [rbx+0x130]        ; &inbound-connection refcount (arg1[0x26])
test    eax, eax
je      0x1c00060a0            ; predicate==0 -> guard-BYPASS accept branch
; ... refcount CAS: lock cmpxchg qword [rdi], rcx ...
; accept dispatch reached at label 0x1c000600b:
movups  xmm0, xmmword [r15+0x88]
mov     eax, dword [r15+0xa8]
movups  xmm1, xmmword [r15+0x98]
lea     rcx, [rbp-0x49]
movups  xmmword [rbp+0x7], xmm0
lea     r8d, [rdx+0x50]
movups  xmmword [rbp+0x17], xmm1
call    0x1c000d740
mov     rcx, qword [rbx+0x160]
lea     rax, [rbp+0x7]
mov     qword [rbp-0x39], rax
lea     rdx, [rbp-0x49]
lea     rax, [r15+0x1e0]
mov     qword [rbp-0x29], r15
mov     qword [rbp-0x31], rax
lea     rax, [rel 0x1c000e000]  ; &VmbusTlProviderConnectDispatch
mov     qword [rbp-0x21], rax
call    qword [rel 0x1c00152b0] ; (*(arg1[0x2a]+8))(arg1[0x2c], &var_a8)  <-- ACCEPT CALLBACK, no guard on je-branch
mov     esi, eax
test    eax, eax
js      0x1c0006169
cmp     byte [rbx+0x128], 0x0   ; listener accept-guard (arg1[0x25]) -- enforced ONLY on non-bypass branch
jne     0x1c0005fda            ; -> error 0xC0000236
; PATCHED: single path always executes cmp byte [rbx+0x128],0 / jne 0x1c0005d18 BEFORE the accept callback

breakpoints.why: rcx (arg1) = listener/endpoint object; watch byte [rcx+0x128] (accept-guard flag) and qword [rcx+0x130] (inbound-connection refcount). rdx (arg2) = incoming connection descriptor.

Branch target taken when EvaluateCurrentState()==0 (Velocity feature disabled). On this pre-patch branch the accept path proceeds without the byte[endpoint+0x128] guard check; the patched build has no such branch.

Indirect call performing (*(*(endpoint+0x150)+8))(*(endpoint+0x160), &localbuf) on the reference-held endpoint. This is the listener's legitimate accept-dispatch callback; inspect endpoint+0x150/+0x160 to confirm they are live object fields (not a proven freed object).

watch: byte [rbx+0x128] = accept-guard flag (arg1[0x25]); expect 0 on safe path, non-zero when bug is armed
qword [rbx+0x130] = inbound-connection refcount (arg1[0x26]) incremented by the CAS loop
qword [rbx+0x150] (arg1[0x2a]) = accept-dispatch object; [that+8] is the called function pointer
qword [rbx+0x160] (arg1[0x2c]) = context passed to the accept callback
eax after `call 0x1c0001d78` (EvaluateCurrentState) — value 0 selects the guard-bypass branch
key_offsets: call 0x1c0001d78 @ start of VmbusTlNotifyListenerAccept = the opaque state predicate
test eax,eax; je 0x1c00060a0 = the branch that leads to the guard-bypassing accept path
cmp byte [rbx+0x128], 0x0 = the listener accept-guard check present only on the non-bypass branch (patched moves it to the single common path @0x1c0005d18)
call qword [rel 0x1c00152b0] = the accept-dispatch callback invocation (sink)
lock cmpxchg qword [rdi], rcx with rdi=[rbx+0x130] = inbound-connection refcount CAS
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Host: create an AF_HYPERV (HV_PROTOCOL_RAW) listening socket bound to a service GUID and listen(). Guest: connect() to that service; the inbound connection is processed and completed host-side via VmbusTlInboundConnectComplete -> VmbusTlNotifyListenerAccept. Bring the listener into the not-accepting/closing state so byte[endpoint+0x128] is set. This exercises a behavior difference, not a race the attacker must win.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Pre-patch feature-disabled path: the accept-dispatch callback fires despite byte[endpoint+0x128] being set. Pre-patch feature-enabled and post-patch unconditionally: VmbusTlNotifyListenerAccept returns 0xC0000236 (STATUS_CONNECTION_REFUSED) and the callback does not fire. Do NOT expect a freed-pool value at poi(endpoint+0x150)+8 — the endpoint is reference-held across the callback; the original report's freed-pool/bugcheck expectation is unsubstantiated by the diff.
debugging_aids.notes: Endpoint object uses pool tag 0x69706e56 ('Vnpi'). Fields: +0x08 object refcount; +0x130 inbound-connection refcount (try-referenced, object alive while >= 1); +0x128 accept-guard flag; +0x150 accept-dispatch object ([+8] = fptr); +0x160 callback context; +0x1a9 accepting-state byte (checked in VmbusTlProcessNewConnectionForListener). EvaluateCurrentState() @ 0x1c0001d78 is Velocity feature 1542768955_57582472, removed binary-wide in the patched build. Distinguish behavior by whether `cmp byte [rbx+0x128],0` executes before the accept-dispatch call.

security_findings.entry_point: Guest→host inbound VMBus hvsocket connect request via VmbusTlProcessNewConnection
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Endpoint accepting-state validation made unconditional via removal of a Velocity feature gate (defense-in-depth hardening)
security_findings.description: Companion feature-gate fold to the VmbusTlNotifyListenerAccept change. In the unpatched build the endpoint accepting-state byte (endpoint+0x1a9) is read only when the Velocity feature EvaluateCurrentState() is enabled, and the refusal `if (EvaluateCurrentState() && byte==0)` (trace 588) only fires when the feature is enabled; the create path otherwise depends only on byte[endpoint+0xb9]. The patched build removes the feature gate: it reads endpoint+0x1a9 unconditionally under the fast mutex and hard-requires it (`if (byte==0) return 0xC0000236`, trace 572/579) before VmbusTlCreateConnection. This makes the accepting-state requirement permanent. Same feature (1542768955_57582472) as the primary finding.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: No timing trigger; the patch changes a default behavior via feature-gate removal. With the feature disabled (pre-patch) an inbound connection can be created/associated for an endpoint whose accepting-state byte[endpoint+0x1a9] is clear; with the feature enabled (pre-patch) and unconditionally (post-patch) the driver returns 0xC0000236 and no connection is created.
security_findings.primitive: Creation/association of an inbound connection against an endpoint whose accepting-state byte is clear on the pre-patch feature-disabled path; a state/lifecycle-consistency gap on guest input, now refused. No memory-corruption primitive is evidenced by the diff.
security_findings.evidence:
// unpatched: r12 = *(rbx_4+0x1a9) only if EvaluateCurrentState(); gate = if (!EvaluateCurrentState() || r12) { if(!r14){...VmbusTlCreateConnection...} }
// patched:  rdi = *(r14+0x1a9);  if (rdi) { if(!r12){...VmbusTlCreateConnection...} else err } else err 0xc0000236

breakpoints.why: arg1 (rcx) = provider/partition context; var_50 = referenced listener endpoint. After the mutex block inspect byte [endpoint+0x1a9] (accepting-state) and the branch selecting VmbusTlCreateConnection.
label_1c001a268 = VmbusTlCreateConnection call site reached after the gate. Confirm byte[endpoint+0x1a9] state when this is reached in the unpatched build vs. patched.

watch: endpoint object pointer (var_50) fetched via VmbusTlFindAndReferencePartition
*(endpoint+0xb9) and *(endpoint+0xba) = state fields read under mutex
key_offsets: label_1c001a268 = VmbusTlCreateConnection entry to inbound-connection creation
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Same as primary: host AF_HYPERV listener + guest connect() flood, race host-side endpoint teardown to toggle byte[endpoint+0x1a9] while inbound connects are processed.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Unpatched: VmbusTlCreateConnection proceeds and the connection is associated even when byte[endpoint+0x1a9] is clear; patched: returns 0xC0000236 (trace ordinal 0x23c/0x243) and no connection is created.
debugging_aids.notes: This function shares the endpoint object and refcount conventions (+0x08 object refcount, +0x130 inbound-connection count, tag 'Vnpi' 0x69706e56). It is the caller-side companion to the VmbusTlNotifyListenerAccept fix.

summary: The patch to hvsocket.sys (Hyper-V Socket / VMBus transport) is the removal of a single Windows Feature-Management (Velocity) staging gate, feature 1542768955_57582472, evaluated via EvaluateCurrentState() (sub_1c0001d78; EvaluateFeature -> EvaluateCurrentStateFromRegistry reads ...\Microsoft\FeatureManagement\Overrides). In the unpatched build each of the 9 changed functions has two twin paths selected by `if (EvaluateCurrentState())`: a feature-enabled path with stricter refcount/lock/state handling and a feature-disabled path with the older looser handling. The patched build deletes the gate at every call site and empties the driver's feature-descriptor table (the init loop iterates &reg_FeatureDescriptors_z..&reg_FeatureDescriptors_z, an empty range), keeping only the feature-enabled (safer) path everywhere. Per the classification rule, a removed feature gate that folds all callers to the safe path binary-wide is a DELIVERED fix. The most security-relevant delivered effect is on the guest-reachable inbound-accept path: VmbusTlNotifyListenerAccept (0x1c0005f18 -> 0x1c0005c68) now always checks the listener accept-guard byte at endpoint+0x128 before dispatching the accept callback (returning 0xC0000236 when set), and VmbusTlProcessNewConnectionForListener (0x1c0019e60 -> 0x1c0019db0) now always requires the endpoint accepting-state byte at endpoint+0x1a9 before VmbusTlCreateConnection. The other seven functions fold to safer refcount/lock handling on the in-host (host-local) loopback path. CORRECTION vs the original automated report: this is NOT a use-after-free, controlled indirect call, or guest-to-host VM escape. The endpoint is reference-held across the accept callback (a try-reference that only proceeds when the refcount is >= 1), and endpoint+0x128 is a logical accept-not-allowed/closing flag, not a liveness marker. The report's UAF/RCE/pool-grooming/KASLR/ROP/HVCI-bypass narrative is unsubstantiated by the diff and was removed. The proven primitive is a guest-reachable state-validation gap now enforced unconditionally: max severity Low. The default enablement state of the feature in the unpatched build, and the exact Windows build/KB, are not resolvable from the decompilation.
